scope_note: Dartmouth does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff, an admitted-student GPA distribution, or any GPA breakdown by major. Their cited admissions guidance says transcript context and grade trends matter more than a single numeric cutoff.
policy_summary: Dartmouth does not state a minimum required GPA on its cited admissions pages. Dartmouth says a top secondary school GPA is useful, but it evaluates grade trends and transcript context rather than using GPA alone as a cutoff.

what_mit_does_say.claim: Dartmouth does not publish a minimum GPA cutoff for first-year applicants on its cited admissions pages.
Dartmouth says a top secondary school GPA is only part of how it evaluates applicants, and that it reads each transcript and school profile closely.
Dartmouth says applicants typically pursue the most rigorous coursework available to them, especially in their areas of greatest interest.

recommended_gpa.summary: Dartmouth does not publish HS GPA data for its incoming class. The numbers below are third-party estimates, not official Dartmouth figures. Use them as directional references, not cutoffs.

estimates.label: Weighted (estimate)
estimates.method: Estimated using data from over 1,000 schools, because Dartmouth did not officially report its average GPA.

estimates.label: Unweighted (prediction)
estimates.method: Predicted by a CollegeSimply algorithm using Dartmouth's test scores and a large sample of college GPA data, because Dartmouth has not published HS GPA data for incoming freshmen.

recommended_gpa.interpretation: Both estimates indicate a transcript full of top grades. The weighted 4.11 suggests many competitive applicants combine A-range marks with advanced courses; the unweighted 3.90 is roughly an A/A- average.
